rationale: Pure boundary is the regex predicate `s => /protect-mcp (evaluate|sign)\b/.test(s)`. Derivable property: ∀s, OBSOLETE.test(s) ↔ s contains `protect-mcp evaluate` OR `protect-mcp sign` (as a word). L0.audit-1 instantiates this with the canonical 2-tuple (positive, negative); the property holds. Counterexamples would require a string matching the regex but NOT containing those substrings — impossible by regex definition.
